english: Comp. rǰéd-ṅas-čan (rjed ngas can) Lexx. (मुषितस्मृति) forgetful, oblivious; Cs. gives inst. of it: rǰéd-ṅes-čan (rjed nges can), but also thus no clear etymological explanation is obtained. - rǰéd-ču (rjed chu) draught of oblivion, of Lethe Cs. - rǰed-bsnyén (rjed bsnyen) (etymology?) sgúg-pa (sgug pa) technical term for the common practice of Indian servants to hide an object belonging to their master in some obscure corner, and after waiting (sgúg-pa (sgug pa)) for some months, until it may be assumed that the thing is altogether forgotten (brǰéd-pa (brjed pa)), to appropriate it to themselves. - rǰéd-to (rjed tho) list of notes, memorandum-book, journal, diary, cashbook etc. Glr., C., W. - rǰed-rdó (rjed rdo) prob. monumental or memorial stone. - rǰed-byáṅ (rjed byang) specifications or lists of goods, pieces of luggage etc. which the Tibetans number and mark with the letters of the alphabet. - rǰed-byéd (rjed byed) 1. a demon that takes away the power of memory, also rǰed-byéd-kyi rdon (rjed byed kyi gdon). 2. epilepsy (अपस्मार) Med. - rǰed-zás (rjed zas) Cs.: 'the meat of forgetfulness'.

1. not covered, bare, naked, B., C. (W.: *čer-nyál* (cer nyal)), rkaṅ-rǰén (-pa) (rkang rjen (-pa)) barefooted, unshod; ẓabs-rǰén-par ɣdá-ba (zhabs rjen par gda' ba) or ɣšégs-pa (gshegs pa), resp., to be barefooted, to go barefoot; ɣdoṅ rǰén-du sdód-pa (gdong rjen du sdod pa) to sit with unveiled face, mgo-rǰén-pa (mgo rjen pa) with uncovered head, rgyab-rǰén (rgyab rjen) with a naked back Cs.; rǰén-par _dón-pa (rjen par 'don pa) C. to strip perfectly; dmar-rǰén (dmar rjen) stark naked Sch.; rál-gri rǰén-pa (ral gri rjen pa) a naked sword; *žen-pa toṅ* (zhen pa tong) W. give it (me) not wrapped up! sa-rǰén (sa rjen) the bare ground, not covered with a carpet Cs.; rǰén-ne-ba (rjen ne ba) undisguised, obvious to the understanding, manifest Mil. - 2. raw, not roasted or cooked, ša-rǰen (sha rjen) raw meat, dmar-rǰén (dmar rjen) red raw meat; mar-rǰén (mar rjen) not melted butter; nas-rǰén (nas rjen) raw barley, not prepared or roasted; also the meal of it: W. *nar-ǰén* (nar rjen) barley-flour, cf. Sch.: bra-rǰén (bra rjen) buckwheat-meal. - rǰen-zás (rjen zas) Med. (Cs. also rǰen-rígs (rjen rigs)) victuals that may be eaten raw. - 3. not ripe, unripe W.

1. trace, track, mark left, impression made (on the ground), pyí-rǰés (phyi rjes) Med. prob. id.; mi-rǰés (mi rjes) a man's track, rta-rǰés (rta rjes) a horse's track Glr.; šiṅ-rtai rǰes (shing rta'i rjes) the track of a waggon or cart, a rut; rkaṅ-rǰés (rkang rjes), resp. ẓabs-rǰés (zhabs rjes), the trace of one's foot, footprint, rkaṅ-rǰés byuṅ (rkang rjes byung) a footprint is made; rkaṅ-rǰés ǰóg-pa (rkang rjes jog pa) to leave a footprint behind Mil.; byas-rǰés (byas rjes) proof of an accomplished deed, whether it be the work itself or some indubitable result of it; lag-rǰés (lag rjes), resp. pyag-rǰés (phyag rjes) impression or mark left of one's hand, hence fig.: action, deed, charitable institution, pious legacy, whereby a person wishes to immortalize his name. - 2. the hind part of a thing Sch. (?) - 3. inrelation to time: that which follows, the consequence, the course or progress of a thing, the last, = mǰug (mjug). - 4. adv. and postp. inst. of rǰés-su (rjes su), v. below. - rǰes ɣčód-pa (rjes gcod pa) 1. Sch. to destroy, blot out, efface a track or trace, in Med. to eradicate the trace of a disease, to cure it thoroughly, 2. Sch.: to separate, disjoin the hind part (?) 3. W. *ẓes čád-če* (zhes bcad ce) to follow a trace or track, to find out or to come upon the track. - rǰes dzín-pa (rjes 'dzin pa) to 'seize' the track, to overtake Glr., also to be able to follow the track, rá-ma kyui rǰes mi zín-pa (ra ma khyu'i rjes mi zin pa) a goat that cannot follow the flock Mil. - rǰés-la (rjes la), rǰés-su (rjes su), rǰes (rjes), adv. and postp., afterwards, hereafter, for the future, later; after, behind, deï rǰés-la (de'i rjes la), de-rǰés (de rjes) after that, afterwards, later Mil.; dé-dag _dás-pai rǰés-su (de dag 'das pa'i rjes su) after these were gone Glr.; bẓag-rǰés po. (bzhag rjes po.) = bẓág-pai _óg-tu (bzhag pa'i 'og tu) Lt.; ṅaï rǰés-su (nga'i rjes su) after my death. rǰés-su (rjes su) in conjunction with verbs corresponds to the Ssk. अनु and is often not to be translated, or serves only to give additional force to some other word or expression: rǰés-su _gró-ba (rjes su 'gro ba), _bráṅ-ba ('brang ba) to go after, to follow, to come after; also fig.: spyód-pa ťams-čád ya-rábs-kyi rǰés-su _bróṅ-ba (spyod pa thams cad ya rabs kyi rjes su 'brong ba) to imitate the nobility, the free-born, in their whole demeanour Glr.; lé-lo daṅ spyód-pa ṅán-pai rǰés-su _gró-ba (le lo dang spyod pa ngan pa'i rjes su 'gro ba) to imitate idleness and wickedness, or idle and wicked
